programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Lisa are forced to get creative when Homer causes an upset on Halloween.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: As Adams girlfriend, Brea, heads to visit her cousin at the University of Virginia, Beverly projects her own insecurity onto Adam, convincing him to make a grand gesture to prevent Brea from choosing UVA for college.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When the NFL players go on strike and the football season is threatened, Barry gets concerned that he and Murray will no longer have anything to talk about; Beverly decides to start selling a nutritional product from a sketchy company.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Mummys boy Tiago plans to put his self-conscious bride Karlette on the big screen at his 1950s drive-in wedding, but she wants something intimate and contemporary.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claire lands an interview for her dream job. Meanwhile, Cam and Mitch debate the pros and cons of adopting another baby.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitchell struggles to let go of his old life, leading to an intervention from Gloria. Haley, Luke and Alex decide to throw a party.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tubes naughty little sister, featuring sports of the lawnmower, motorcycle and bicycle variety.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay continues his travels across the United States in a bid to save failing restaurants from disaster. This week, Gordon visits beachside restaurant Barefoot Bobs in Hull, Massachusetts, owned by husband-and-wife team Marc and Lisa. The duo opened the grill in the summer of 2004 to a busy seafront crowd, but every year the bottom line collapses through the bleak winter months.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Barry and Joanne crash Erica and Geoffs peaceful weekend camping trip, they end up in a competition to see who is the better couple; Adam seeks to prove to Brea that he doesnt have an easy, privileged life.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly involves herself in Erica and Barrys relationships; Adam and Brea are cast opposite of each other in the school play, but their excitement for one anothers success grows dim when one starts to outshine the other onstage.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay accidentally gatecrashes Mitch and Cams evening out, and Luke befriends his crotchety old neighbour.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Cam accepts the role of music director at Luke and Mannys school, but his enthusiasm is far from infectious. Meanwhile, Jays brother Donnie is in town.

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The Couples Retreat continues amid a sour atmosphere, with Becs comments from the first night fuelling a venomous divide in the group.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: First into the restaurant is 36-year-old, larger-than-life stand-up comedian Mani. His match is 30-year-old nursing home owner Natasha, whose quick wit and dry sense of humour are sure to give Mani a run for his money.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits Cafe Hon in Hampden, Maryland, Chiarellas in Philadelphia, and Leones in Montclair, New Jersey, to see whether the restaurant owners have taken his advice on board.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer sees his chance to live the good life when Lisa pals up with a mega-rich new girl at her school.

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay is determined to help Manny sell wrapping paper for a school fundraiser, and Claire petitions the city for a stop sign to be installed at a high-traffic intersection.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: There must be something in the air because all the men in the family are angry. While Jay is frustrated at work, Mitch and Cam are involved in a fender bender.

programme.title: Americas Next Top Model

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Bart befriends a famous kid influencer who owns an ultra-cool skate wear brand, Homer leads a rebellion of loser dads against them.

programme.title: Mister Lonely
programme.desc: A disillusioned Michael Jackson impersonator finds faith and love when he meets a Marilyn Monroe impersonator who takes him to a remote Scottish island where impersonators are free to live their lives.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: 3:10 to Yuma
programme.desc: A drought-devastated farmer is desperate for money and, for $200, agrees to guard a notorious outlaw until the train comes to take him to jail. However, the farmer has not reckoned on his prisoners psychological wiles and must resist manipulative strategies aimed at permitting him to escape.

programme.title: The Tall Men
programme.desc: Two brothers and their pompous employer contend with multiple hazards as they drive cattle across the rugged frontier.

programme.title: They Rode West
programme.desc: An Army outpost doctor clashes with his commander when he attempts to treat a band of malaria-stricken Indians.

programme.title: The Desert Fox
programme.desc: A portrait of Field Marshal Erwin Rommel, beginning with his initially successful exploits in the North African desert until his defeat by Montgomery. Returning to Berlin, he becomes involved with Colonel Von Stauffenbergs plot to assassinate Hitler - but when the dictator escapes the bomb and the conspirators are rounded up, Rommel, because of his high standing with the German public, is offered the choice of suicide.

programme.title: Deep Impact
programme.desc: Apocalyptic thriller in which life on Earth is threatened by a rogue comet. When a reporter in pursuit of a Washington sex scandal is arrested by the FBI, she discovers that the story is an inconsequential part of a huge cover-up. She soon finds herself in possession of the biggest story in the history of humankind - the planet will be destroyed in one year, unless a risky outer-space mission can deflect the comet.

programme.title: Jennifers Body
programme.desc: In a bid to further their careers, indie band Low Shoulder made a pact with the Devil: one that requires the soul of a virgin. They kidnap and kill cheerleader Jennifer, but unfortunately she was no virgin and rises again to take her retribution.

programme.title: Dead Mans Shoes
programme.desc: Set in a small, bleak Derbyshire town. After seven years in the army, Richard returns to find out what happened to his beloved brother Anthony, who latched on to the local drugs cartel and became the butt of their cruelty due to his learning difficulties. As Richard begins to take revenge against the gang, led by Sonny, the story of Anthonys life and fate unfolds through flashback and revelations.

programme.title: Let Him Go
programme.desc: Following the loss of their son, a retired sheriff and his wife leave their Montana ranch to rescue their young grandson from the clutches of a dangerous family living off the grid in the Dakotas.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood City Bakes
programme.desc: The second series of Paul Hollywood City Bakes takes viewers on a journey to even more of the worlds best baking destinations, where Paul will meet the crème de la crème of bakers and continues his culinary education.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick visits Cork in Southern Ireland. At the English Market, the gastronomic heart of the city, he talks to retailers and cooks corned beef and cabbage. Still in Eire, he travels up the east coast to Waterford, a town usually renowned for its glass.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick is on home turf when he visits the Tintagel area in Cornwall. Here, legend has it, the ruined castle atop the craggy, untamed cliffs is what remains of King Arthurs Camelot.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummonds sharing recipes from her pizza place in town. For takeout with a difference, she makes fool-proof pizza dough, garlic butter and a no-cook pizza sauce, and uses them for her signature pie, Pizza Ree-a.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: The Drummond family is throwing a farewell dinner for college-bound cousins Paige and Caleb. Rees menu includes brown butter mashed potatoes, roasted cauliflower salad, Drummond-grilled rib eyes and strawberry shortcake trifle.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver offers a clever spin on the much-loved cheese and beans combo with Jamies spicy, smoky BBQ baked beans with sweet potatoes. Theres scrumptious lamb biryani and a Vietnamese-style salad.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver cooks piri piri pork belly with crispy crackling. Theres also a meat-free roast using celeriac, creamy mushroom sauce and cheap-as-chips pearl barley; and grilled garlic mussels with sweet tomato soup.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina Gartens husband, Jeffrey, takes a pizza making class and learns to make a white pizza, while Ina prepares a platter of rocket with prosciutto and burrata, and a limoncello ricotta cheesecake to go with it.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cooking for Jeffrey: It Must Be Friday
programme.desc: Its Friday, which means Jeffreys coming home and its chicken for dinner! Ina shakes things up with skillet-roasted lemon chicken with roasted broccolini, then follows with raspberry rhubarb crostata with ice cream for dessert.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The Bake Off begins with Cake Week, and sees the bakers take on a fruity signature challenge. Next, Prue sets her first technical - a childrens tea party favourite - and everyone is out to impress the new judge.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: One of the most popular television programmes in the United Kingdom returns for an eighth season as twelve new budding bakers compete to be crowned the champion. Established judge Paul Hollywood is joined by food connoisseur Prue Leith, while seasoned entertainers Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding take on the role of being the shows new hosts. Despite the change in personnel, the key Bake Off traditions remain intact, as the contestants try to impress the panel with a fine selection of beautifully-crafted and delicious cakes, tarts, breads and more.

programme.title: James Martin Delicious
programme.desc: James applies his know-how to barbecued spare ribs, pork terrine and roast belly pork. He also visits Andover farmers market to find the best local ingredients.

programme.title: James Martin Delicious
programme.desc: James prepares olive oil focaccia with rosemary, anchovy pizza and poppy seed snap visits De Gustibus bakery in London to get some tips from a master baker.

programme.title: James Martin Delicious
programme.desc: James Martin prepares red onion and creme fraiche pizza, watercress and scallop salad and watercress and Stilton soup. The country around Winchester is famous for watercress, and James visits a grower and learns that the secret is in the water.

programme.title: James Martin Delicious
programme.desc: James prepares brill with boudin and black-eyed beans, halibut parcels with capers and beans and cod with shrimp butter. He also visits Mudderford Quay - famous for its beach huts and the best fish stall in the country.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick visits Cork in Southern Ireland. At the English Market, the gastronomic heart of the city, he talks to retailers and cooks corned beef and cabbage. Still in Eire, he travels up the east coast to Waterford, a town usually renowned for its glass.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick is on home turf when he visits the Tintagel area in Cornwall. Here, legend has it, the ruined castle atop the craggy, untamed cliffs is what remains of King Arthurs Camelot.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummonds sharing recipes from her pizza place in town. For takeout with a difference, she makes fool-proof pizza dough, garlic butter and a no-cook pizza sauce, and uses them for her signature pie, Pizza Ree-a.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: The Drummond family is throwing a farewell dinner for college-bound cousins Paige and Caleb. Rees menu includes brown butter mashed potatoes, roasted cauliflower salad, Drummond-grilled rib eyes and strawberry shortcake trifle.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver offers a clever spin on the much-loved cheese and beans combo with Jamies spicy, smoky BBQ baked beans with sweet potatoes. Theres scrumptious lamb biryani and a Vietnamese-style salad.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver cooks piri piri pork belly with crispy crackling. Theres also a meat-free roast using celeriac, creamy mushroom sauce and cheap-as-chips pearl barley; and grilled garlic mussels with sweet tomato soup.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina Gartens husband, Jeffrey, takes a pizza making class and learns to make a white pizza, while Ina prepares a platter of rocket with prosciutto and burrata, and a limoncello ricotta cheesecake to go with it.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cooking for Jeffrey: It Must Be Friday
programme.desc: Its Friday, which means Jeffreys coming home and its chicken for dinner! Ina shakes things up with skillet-roasted lemon chicken with roasted broccolini, then follows with raspberry rhubarb crostata with ice cream for dessert.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The Bake Off begins with Cake Week, and sees the bakers take on a fruity signature challenge. Next, Prue sets her first technical - a childrens tea party favourite - and everyone is out to impress the new judge.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: One of the most popular television programmes in the United Kingdom returns for an eighth season as twelve new budding bakers compete to be crowned the champion. Established judge Paul Hollywood is joined by food connoisseur Prue Leith, while seasoned entertainers Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding take on the role of being the shows new hosts. Despite the change in personnel, the key Bake Off traditions remain intact, as the contestants try to impress the panel with a fine selection of beautifully-crafted and delicious cakes, tarts, breads and more.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James takes a magical trip to the stunning Isle of Skye, where hes joined by top chef and ultimate Scotsman Tony Singh. Tony visits a local bakery which doubles as an art gallery and learns to make the local oatcakes. James is inspired by the amazing local seafood and cooks up a tasty dish using plaice and lovage. Their day comes to a perfect end aboard a boat, where they spot dozens of dolphins, and James cooks up a fresh lobster and langoustine dish with an added Scottish twist.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James visits a place hes always wanted to see - the beautiful Shetland Islands - and they definitely do not disappoint. James gets to hire a vintage car to explore the area, where he learns about traditional fish salting and cooks a tasty vegetable curry at a local social enterprise project.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.sub-title: You Can Only Get It Here
programme.desc: This trip Guys finding funky flavours all over the country. In San Francisco, an Asian/American/Mexican fusion joint doing a Korean spicy pork burrito with homemade kimchee.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip Guys rolling out for something new from the grill - and more. In San Antonio, Texas, a 60-year-old drive-in where the owners are putting a Thai twist on classic Texas bar-b-que.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip Guys heading all over the country for a taste of the south. In San Francisco, a whisky bar putting their own spin on a po boy with avocado and fresh crab mayo.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip Guys rolling in for farm-to-table cooking. In Boulder, Colorado, a college town favourite since the 1920s, where theyre flipping grass-fed beef burgers.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb journeys across America to tackle daunting food challenges and sample iconic regional dishes. Enormous food options create problems for even the most voracious of eaters.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Crabs, Communion and a Comeback Pastry
programme.desc: Its all hands and claws on deck as a local marina orders a crab cake to kick off the season. However, theres a catch, they want it to move.

programme.title: Cake Boss

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: In the Clovelly area of North Devon, Rick goes fishing for herring with Steve Perham, whose family have been fishermen for over 200 years. All that fresh sea air gives them a hearty appetite as Rick fries the herring in wholemeal flour.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick and Chalky travel to Loch Torridon in Scotland to go fishing with John McGreggor for langoustines. Here the prawns are protected by the Marien Stewardship Council, which has awarded its seal of approval to the fishermen.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is filling her freezer with breakfast favourites and feeding the guys out working cattle. Her to-make list includes freezer breakfast paninis, very berry croissants and frozen yoghurt bark.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond improves her potluck game with four portable ideas for barbecue season. She serves up an eight-layer dip, barbecue potato salad, blueberry cornbread panzanella, and a lemon icebox pie.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a frugal but amazing paella with chicken thighs, spicy chorizo and prawns from the freezer. Theres also crisp Cajun fishcakes with a classic chopped salad and Jamies snake in the hole.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie makes a thrifty curry with chicken drumsticks and frugal but posh beans on toast.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cooking for Jeffrey: Back to School Surprise
programme.desc: Jeffrey is going back to school after a long holiday, so Ina Garten prepares an alfresco dinner of Moroccan grilled lamb chops, couscous with pine nuts and mint, and roasted vine tomatoes.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina is teaching Dinner Party 101 to her great friend, actress Debra Monk. The foolproof meal plan includes roast chicken with bread and rocket salad, a stunning easy cheese board, tri-berry crumbles and a table-setting tutorial.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The curtain raises on the competition in the North West. How will Aiden Byrnes dish entitled `Mad as a Box of Frogs go down with veteran Phil Howard? Will he trounce Mary-Ellen McTagues crab starter or will Chris Hollands `Oriental style beef broth with a twis score more highly with Phil?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North West chefs cook their fish courses. Will Aiden Byrnes `The Culinary Joke - a take on a classic prawn cocktail - raise a laugh, or will Mary-Ellen McTagues dish of dover sole or Chris Hollands `Gone Fishing salmon dish prove winners?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their main course. Will Aiden Byrnes witty beef dish entitled `Long Horn Beef with Noodle Pot prove a winner, or will Mary-Ellen McTagues goat dish called `Our Kid raise a smile? Perhaps Chris Holland will score highly with his dish called `A Tasting of Pork.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the chefs last chance to impress before the final two are chosen to face the `Great British Menu judging panel. Which dessert will help get them through? Will it be Aiden Byrnes pineapple dish called `The Sweet Burger, Mary-Ellen McTagues take on a trifle called `Chamomile Lawn or Chris Hollands dish called `Going Bananas?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its decision time for the chefs from the North West. One chef has already left the competition and only two remain. The judges are joined by celebrity guest judge writer, presenter and performer Rowland Rivron.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: In central England, three culinary heavyweights return to the `Great British Menu kitchen. Two Michelin star chef Daniel Clifford creates a dish called `A Trip to the Chippy, where nothing is as it first appears. That is also the theme for Michelin star chef Richard Bainbridges starter, `banana split, while Will Holland offers up goats cheese mousse, balsamic jelly and gingerbread.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Here, the chefs try to impress with their fish courses. Daniel Clifford puts up a creative dish called `Catch of the Day. Does Richard Bainbridges dish titled `Tongue in Cheek live up to its comedy name? Will judge Marcus Wareing find the comedy value in Will Hollands coconut poached brill, crab, celeriac and tomato essence?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs in central England cook their main courses. Daniel Cliffords main has the same title as Richard Bainbridges fish course, `Tongue in Cheek, but it is a wildly different plate of food; Richard Bainbridge presents guinea fowl in a beef bladder and calls the dish `Which Came First: the Chicken or the Egg; Will Holland offers roast longhorn beef rib eye, bread and bone marrow pudding and parsley paste.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs in central England have one last chance to impress Marcus Wareing before the final two are chosen to face the judging panel. Will Daniel Cliffords `Going Out with a Bang be good enough? Has Richard Bainbridge dropped the comedy ball with his coffee and chocolate dessert called `Time to Reflect? Or will an unusual dish of parsnip banoffee cake, chocolate tart and parsnip ice cream get Will Holland through?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its decision time for the chefs from the central England region. Only two chefs remain to cook their entire menus for judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The panel is joined by guest judge, comedian and actor Jim Moir, known to millions as Vic Reeves.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs battle it out for a chance to cook at the special banquet. Will veteran judge Tom Kerridge like Chris Edens modern twist on `Duck a lOrange, or will he favour Peter Sanchez-Iglesias dish called `Picnic? Perhaps Emily Watkinss witty dish called `Eggs and Ham will prove a winner.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs cook their fish courses here. Cornishman Chris Eden takes inspiration from home to create a lobster and oyster dish called `Gone Surfing, Peter Sanchez-Iglesias cooks up a dish inspired by childhood memories titled `Fish and Chips, while Emily Watkins serves up a dish with cured trout called `High Tea.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs cook their main courses and veteran Tom Kerridge is looking for perfection. Will Chris Edens spin on a fast food classic simply called `B.L.T gain top points? Or will Peter Sanchez Iglesias nostalgic dish `Roast Dinner or Emily Watkins venison dish `Shoot Lunch prove a winner?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: For the South West chefs, which dessert will progress? Peach melba, apple pie and `Lolly and Sweets fight it out.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its decision time for the chefs from the South West and only two remain. Who will cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton and their guest, writer and performer Simon Day? The judges arent easily pleased and only world-class cooking will do.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs raise the curtain with their starters, but which one is going to produce a showstopper? All three contestants have created sophisticated plates of food with simple dish titles. Will veteran Jeremy Lee be impressed with Richard Daviess chicken salad or will Mary Ann Gilchrists peas and ham or Luke Thomass ham, egg and pineapple get them off to a flying start?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs cook their fish course. Richard Davies serves up grilled and tartare of mackerel, while Mary Ann Gilchrist serves a lobster dish and calls it `The Great Escape. Luke Thomas is hoping that his quirky take on a classic fish finger will impress veteran Jeremy Lee too.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs prepare their main courses, but will any of them impress veteran Jeremy Lee enough to give them a top score? Richard Daviess dish is simply called `A Tasting of Pork, Mary Ann Gilchrists `Threes a Crowd doesnt give much away, while Luke Thomass dish is called `Beef, Bone Marrow and Watercress.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the final chance for the Welsh chefs to impress Jeremy Lee with their dessert dishes. Which creation will help get two chefs through to the judging show? Will it be Richard Daviess strawberries and cream with a Comic Relief twist, or will Mary Ann Gilchrists `Some Like It Hot or Luke Thomass rhubarb and custard get them through?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Great British Menu continues as 24 of the nations finest chefs battle it out to cook gastronomic masterpieces that are also witty and fun. Their dishes have to put a smile on faces as they compete for the prize of cooking a dish at a special banquet at the Royal Albert Hall celebrating 25 years of Comic Reliefs Red Nose Day appeals. It is decision time for the chefs representing Wales.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three North East chefs all want to top the leader board on day one with their starter. How will Colin McGurrans tomato gazpacho and garden salad perform? Stephanie Moon thinks she also has a chance with a witty chicken dish called `Why Did the Chicken..., and Charlie Lakin offers a controversial duck dish called `Mind Your Step.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North East chefs cook their fish courses. Will former champion Colin McGurrans dish of cockles and mussels with salmon, inspired by his childhood memories, also inspire veteran judge Jason Atherton? Or perhaps Stephanie Moons fish dish with a nod to one of the most famous gags of all time, crab custard pie, will be the winner? Charlie Lakin is also confident with his creation called `Tongue in Cheek.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North East chefs cook their main course and veteran Jason Atherton is looking for perfection. Will Colin McGurrans dish of duck and turnips be funnier than it sounds? Has Stephanie Moon dropped the comedy ball with her venison dish called `Behind the Fun is Some Serious Game? Will Charlie Lakins kebab inspired dish `A Night on the Tiles raise a smile?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the North East chefs last chance to impress before the final two are chosen to face the `Great British Menu judging panel. Which desserts will help get two chefs through to the showdown? Will it be Colin McGurrans tiramisu, Stephanie Moons tricky pulled sugar dome, or Charlie Larkins custard pie?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its decision time for the chefs from the North East. One chef has already left the competition and only two remain. They must now cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and their guest, comedian Tim Brooke-Taylor. The judges arent easily pleased and only world class cooking will do.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The curtain raises on the competition in Northern Ireland. How will Chris Fearon cope competing against his former head chef Raymond McArdle? Will rising star Ian Orr be caught up in the middle? Both Chris and Raymond create very different takes on soup while Ian comes up with a rabbit dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three Northern Ireland chefs cook their fish courses. Chris Fearon serves up a creation called `Stitched up Like a Kipper, while Raymond McArdle draws inspiration from a view from home and serves turbot called `A Day at the Beach. Ian Orr creates a dish with a play on an old joke called `Why Did the Scallop Cross the Roe.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its time for the main course. Chris Fearons dish called `Cowboy Supper is inspired by the Wild West. Raymond McArdle creates a dish called `Titanic Smoked Venison, Tongue and Cheek, and Ian Orr cooks lamb and baked hay.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the chefs last chance to impress before the final two are chosen to face the `Great British Menu judging panel. Which dessert will it be? Chris Fearons dish inspired by comedy legend Tommy Cooper called `Just Like That? competes against Raymond McArdles `Chocolate Volcano Mountain and Ian Orrs `Passion Egg.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its decision time for the chefs from Northern Ireland. One chef has left the competition and only two remain. Who will cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton? They will be joined by celebrity guest judge writer and performer Charlie Higson and the judges arent easily pleased.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs raise the curtain with their starters, but which one is going to produce a showstopper? All three contestants have created sophisticated plates of food with simple dish titles. Will veteran Jeremy Lee be impressed with Richard Daviess chicken salad or will Mary Ann Gilchrists peas and ham or Luke Thomass ham, egg and pineapple get them off to a flying start?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs cook their fish course. Richard Davies serves up grilled and tartare of mackerel, while Mary Ann Gilchrist serves a lobster dish and calls it `The Great Escape. Luke Thomas is hoping that his quirky take on a classic fish finger will impress veteran Jeremy Lee too.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course and rabbit, rose veal and venison are all dished up for `Great British Menu veteran Richard Corrigan. Plus, one of the chefs is joined by `Eastenders star June Brown for a fundraising challenge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course. Will a strawberry and coconut dessert, a frozen white chocolate dish or apple and cherries come out on top? `Great British Menu veteran judge Richard Corrigan will decide who goes through to the judges tomorrow.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is decision time for the chefs from London and the South East. One chef left the competition last time and only two remain. They will cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. This time they will be joined by celebrity guest judge, comedienne and writer Arabella Weir.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is finals week and 24 of the nations finest chefs have been whittled down to just eight. Over the course of four hour-long shows, they will battle it out in the kitchen for the chance to get a dish to the Comic Relief Banquet. Actress Patricia Hodge, one of the stars of sitcom `Miranda, joins judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort to taste all eight winning regional starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their fish courses. They are all vying to finish in the top three and still be in with a chance of cooking at the special banquet. Tensions are high but there are still plenty of laughs as new dishes are introduced under competition rules, including a Sicilian influenced dish called the `Cod Father. Impressionist Ronni Ancona joins the judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Comedian Ade Edmondson tucks into the chefs main courses to help decide which three will remain in contention to cook at the special banquet. However, the chefs are in for a major surprise as the judges deliver unexpected news about the days competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its judgement day as the chefs not only learn the top three desserts but the judges announce the winning menu. `Fast Show writer and performer Charlie Higson helps judge the puddings. Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort decide on the four winning dishes, but there is a problem - they are torn between two dishes for one of the courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the climax of the competition as the winning chefs cook at the Comic Relief Banquet at the Royal Albert Hall. Special guest and co-founder of the charity Richard Curtis welcomes guests - the unsung heroes of charity work, as well as those who have benefited from their help and some of the celebrities who have inspired us to do something funny for money.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: After the retreat from Dunkirk, Churchill rallied Britain in its darkest hour. Hitler was only just across the channel, but he reckoned without our supreme strategist, the might of the navy and the manoeuvrability of the air force.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Explores Hitlers assault on the Soviet Union in the summer of 1941, Operation Barbarossa and his attack on the Caucasus in 1942.

programme.title: Heinrich Muller - Head of the Gestapo
programme.desc: Heinrich Muller, WWI veteran and Freikorps member, rose to power and aligned with Adolf Hitler.

programme.title: Hitlers Lost Battles
programme.desc: A look at the strategic decisions made by Hitler during the Battle of Britain, which led to Operation Barbarossa, and the Axis invasion of the Soviet Union.

programme.title: Hitlers Lost Battles
programme.desc: The poor military decisions made by Hitler started to become clear by 1942. America got involved in the conflict as a reaction to his declaration of war, as they made ground in North Africa.

programme.title: Great American Generals - Patton, MacArthur, Eisenhower, Bradley
programme.desc: A compilation of archive footage provides insight into what made Patton, MacArthur, Eisenhower, and Bradley heroes of American history.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: The attack on Pearl Harbour brought the sleeping giant, America, into the war, and saw the emergence of the aircraft carrier as the pre-eminent weapon of the seas.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Mussolini wanted to recreate the Roman Empire, but proved to be a poor military strategist and his incompetence contributed to Hitlers eventual defeat.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: War on the two fronts caught up with Hitler leaving him on the run in mid 1943.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: By early 1944, Hitler had long been expecting an Allied attack on north-western France, but his problem was knowing when and where it would come. This episode takes a look at the Allied operations Bodyguard and Overload.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Concentrates on Hitlers attempts in the latter end of 1944 to shift the balance of power back in his favour.

programme.title: Secrets of the Churchill War Rooms
programme.desc: In this film Dan Snow pays a visit to the Churchill War Rooms, walking in the footsteps of the great man.

programme.title: May 1940: Churchills Finest Hour
programme.desc: For the 80th anniversary of Britains darkest hour Dan Snow visits the House of Commons to learn about the speeches that turned the tide of WWII.

programme.title: King Edward I - Longshanks From Braveheart
programme.desc: King Edward I, born in 1239, was raised in a time of political turmoil and martial abilities.

programme.title: The Endurance: Shackletons Legendary Antarctic Expedition
programme.desc: Explorer Ernest Shackleton and his team become stranded for nearly two years in severe antarctic conditions. Narrated by Liam Neeson.

programme.title: The Rise of Napoleon Bonaparte A Military Prodigy
programme.desc: Napoleon Bonaparte, born in 1769 in Corsica, came from minor nobility and claimed Roman heritage.

programme.title: 1805: The Battle of Austerlitz
programme.desc: Recounting an important day in December of 1805, which became one of the most decisive battles and the greatest victory of the Napoleonic era.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: The war in the Pacific against Japan was a savage hand-to-hand, island-to-island struggle, fought by new specialist fighting units like the valiant Chindits.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Allied forces converge on Germany, uncovering the horrors of Belsen and Buchenwald, before pushing towards Berlin, where Hitler clings to power in his last desperate days.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: With Germany beaten, the Allies turned their attention to Japan, who made them pay for every inch of gained ground. How was this last, great foe overcome?

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: War on the two fronts caught up with Hitler leaving him on the run in mid 1943.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: By early 1944, Hitler had long been expecting an Allied attack on north-western France, but his problem was knowing when and where it would come. This episode takes a look at the Allied operations Bodyguard and Overload.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Concentrates on Hitlers attempts in the latter end of 1944 to shift the balance of power back in his favour.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Concentrates on Hitlers attempts in the latter end of 1944 to shift the balance of power back in his favour.

programme.title: Ghost Ships
programme.desc: For more than 200 years, tales of a Phantom Ship have surrounded the waters of Pictou, Nova Scotia: a mysterious ship engulfs in flames and suddenly vanishes. Recently, a charred wooden wreck was discovered in the middle of Pictou Harbour.

programme.title: Invasion: The Normans
programme.desc: In 12th-century Ireland, provincial kings vie for supremacy. When two of these power players drive a rival into exile, he seeks help from Henry II, the Anglo-Norman king of England. Henrys vassal Strongbow and other Norman allies then invade Ireland with frightening power.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Historian Dan Jones is in the heart of Snowdonia exploring a stronghold built not only as an impenetrable fortress but also as a lavish royal palace fit for a fairytale - Caernarfon Castle.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Skeletons tell of both violent death and tenderness at the royal seat of power in Bamburgh, Northumbria, while at a remarkable community project at a shopping centre in Sittingbourne, people curate the grave goods of their own ancestors.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Portsmouth, Alice Roberts learns about Nelsons dark side, the heat of the battle at Trafalgar, the role of the Navy in the British Empire, and the lives of sex workers.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits Elizabethan Plymouth, exploring the story of Englands `Golden Age, from Sir Francis Drake to the Spanish Armada, piracy, theft, and the slave trade.

programme.title: Empire Builders
programme.desc: Dive into the Spanish conquest of Mexico in the 16th century, resulting in 400 years of overlooked history in Latin America.

programme.title: Weird Britain
programme.sub-title: Scotland: The Search For The Loch Ness Monster

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In Norfolk, flint tools unearthed recently push the earliest human occupation back by 200,000 years, to around one million years ago. In Orkney an early farm yields glimpses of our ancestors earliest religious beliefs and customs.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Skeletons tell of both violent death and tenderness at the royal seat of power in Bamburgh, Northumbria, while at a remarkable community project at a shopping centre in Sittingbourne, people curate the grave goods of their own ancestors.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Situated high on a crag overlooking the river Forth, Stirling Castle has guarded the gateway to the Scottish Highlands for centuries. The residence of kings, armies and felons, its battle-scarred walls have witnessed savagery and chivalry, intrigue, alchemy, adultery and murder. Location filming, stunning aerial shots and reconstructions bring to to life its turbulent history.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the turbulent history of Carrickfergus, one of the most ancient castles in Northern Ireland and a stronghold that holds the key to understanding the tempestuous relationship between Britain and Ireland.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores British history through the stories of individual towns and examines Britain in wartime by looking at the coastal town of Dover.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the Georgian era in her hometown of Bristol, from architectural gems to the worlds first chocolate factory, backstreet gin and the horror of the slave trade.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson is out to discover why Pembrokeshire feels so English. There are castles everywhere and it boasts one of the countrys finest cathedrals, sure signs that the Normans stamped their mark all over this little slice of Wales.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson visits the British Channel Islands, which were occupied by Nazi Germany for five years between 1940 and 1945.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Marden Henge: The communal sweat lodges and feasting remains that illuminate the lost rituals of Stonehenge. Durotriges: A glimpse into the bizarre animal sacrifice rituals offered to their gods by a mysterious Celtic tribe of the first century BC.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the most exciting recent archaeological finds in the east of Britain. A team unearths a mass grave, divers search the Thames for clues to a 17th-century tragedy, and a metal detectorist makes the find of a lifetime.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: From ancient Egypt, when the Pharaohs ruled, to Anglo-Saxon England, uncover the origins of the curses surrounding the Bust of Nefertiti, the Staffordshire Hoard and more.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: The hunt leads to Anglo-Saxon England, medieval Europe, China under its first Emperor and Egypt when the Pharaohs still ruled, where the origins of the curses surrounding the Staffordshire Hoard, Black Princes Ruby, and more are uncovered.

programme.title: Weird Britain
programme.desc: Andy is back in Cornwall searching for legends, meeting a real-life mermaid and fishing for Cornwalls infamous sea serpent Morgawr.

programme.title: Weird Britain
programme.desc: Andy explores an ancient cavern, takes a tour of Dartmoors prison museum and uncovers the legend behind Arthur Conan Doyles The Hound of the Baskervilles.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes 45 miles through the Weald in Kent and the Downs of East Sussex to discover the areas rich and surprising Tudor heritage. At the impressively preserved Penshurst Place, author Philippa Gregory helps Tony relish the fate of the Grand Duke of Buckingham at the hands of the young Henry VIII. Tony finishes in Lewes, where he relives one of the more brutal monastic dissolutions.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series Tony takes on a tough four-day trek through the Kintail region of the west Scottish Highlands to discover the story of the Jacobite uprisings of the early 1700s. On three occasions, Highland armies, assisted by the French and the Spanish, attempted to overthrow the King and put a Stuart back on the throne.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets a couple who buy a former millworkers cottage in Derbyshire as their first home together; Lucy Alexander meets a father and daughter team who are seasoned developers buying a house in Kent; and Dion Dublin talks to a man who, along with two friends, has clubbed together to buy an investment property in Lowestoft in Suffolk.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Walsall in the Midlands looking around a 1950s semi; Martin Roberts meets a man developing a ground-floor one-bedroom flat in Ilford in Essex; and Lucy Alexander catches up on a multi-million-pound redevelopment in East Horsley in Surrey where one small cottage with a big garden is converted into eight separate properties.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house with a bathroom layout challenge in Ripley, Derbyshire, a huge three-storey house with its freehold on offer in Erdington, Birmingham, and a mid-terrace house in Londons East Ham in need of a major makeover are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Lincolnshire looking round a pair of rural semi-detached cottages which the new owner may convert into one home. Martin Roberts is in Cheshire at a bungalow with bags of potential, and Lucy Alexander is in Kent talking to friends embarking on their first project together.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house in Chesterton in Staffordshire has a smelly problem and needs a total overhaul. programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A husband and wife duo add their design skills to a house in Croydon in Surrey, while a seasoned developer creates a high-end house in multiple occupation in Coventry and a first time developer also does a great job with a house full of period features in Nutall in Nottinghamshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets one of two young brothers busy building a property portfolio in Bolsover, Derbyshire, while Dion Dublin checks out a three-bedroom semi in Wolverhampton, and Lucy Alexander is impressed by plans to convert a two-bedroom, fire-damaged terraced house in Londons Plaistow into a smart five-bedroom property.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bedroom detached house in Basford in Nottingham, a top floor flat in Portsmouth and a large Edwardian terrace in Crewe are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear about their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A multimillion-pound development in Farningham in Kent, an ambitious, total renovation project in Aspatria in Cumbria and a very small studio flat in Wallington in Surrey are all sold under the hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners to discuss their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Pegswood in Northumberland, a terrace in Swadlincote in Derbyshire and a former shop in Chatham in Kent are given makeovers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Still a Mystery

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates

programme.title: Diabolical: Deadly Love

programme.title: The 1980s: The Deadliest Decade

programme.title: Still a Mystery
programme.desc: She is an innocent bride-to-be; hes a six-year-old schoolboy; both are on the road, away from home, when they disappear.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates
programme.desc: In 1992, a college student disappears after her car breaks down on the motorway. Family and friends join police in a frantic search that hits one setback after another. It may take new advances in DNA technology to finally crack this stubborn case.

programme.title: Diabolical: Deadly Love
programme.desc: Devious masterminds who use their wits and wiles to get away with murder. Law enforcement taps into the methods of these masterminds, aided by insights from forensic psychologists and psychiatrists as well as criminal profilers.

programme.title: Still a Mystery
programme.desc: Two 20-year-olds suffer personal tragedies then have their own lives abruptly snuffed out. Hes shot while hanging out with friends; she disappears, only to turn up dead in a staged suicide. Will their families be able to prove what really happened?

programme.title: Still a Mystery
programme.desc: Two young women are savagely murdered. One has gone fishing only to turn up beaten and drowned, and the other is a pregnant mother attacked while on the job delivering newspapers. Are strangers responsible, or are the culprits much closer to home?

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates
programme.desc: On Thanksgiving Day 2018, in the mountains of Colorado a young pilot goes missing without a trace, leaving behind her baby. Police follow the few clues they have and unravel a story far more bizarre and horrifying than anyone could have imagined.

programme.title: Diabolical: Deadly Love
programme.desc: A burning car is found by the side of a highway on the same night a single mother of two is reported missing. The two events lead investigators to a notorious criminal.

programme.title: The 1980s: The Deadliest Decade
programme.desc: Young actress Rebecca Schaeffer is gunned down in her doorway in Hollywood. Detectives investigate, wondering if theres a copycat killer in their midst.

programme.title: The 1980s: The Deadliest Decade
programme.desc: Thirty-one-year-old violinist Helen Mintiks goes missing during a performance at the Metropolitan Opera House, then her naked corpse is discovered in an air shaft at the opera house.

programme.title: Living a Nightmare
programme.desc: A young man who overcame a devastating brain injury enters a taxi and never resurfaces. Police are at a loss until they receive a mysterious text that pivots the case in a new direction and intensifies the race to bring Julian Williams home alive.

programme.title: Living a Nightmare
programme.desc: Stories of real people who face the surreal horror of losing loved ones to vicious crimes.

programme.title: How (Not) to Get Rid of a Body
programme.desc: Pennsylvania State Troopers are tasked with a haunting murder investigation that takes more than a decade to solve after finding charred and decomposed human remains in the Poconos.

programme.title: A Body in the Basement
programme.desc: On a hot summer night in Springfield, Massachusetts, a seemingly routine traffic stop turns into a nightmare when officers unknowingly apprehend a serial killer and discover a torture chamber and several murdered women in a quaint suburban home.

programme.title: Cabin in the Woods
programme.desc: A fathers desperate search for his daughter leads him to an abandoned cabin in rural Canada; a surprise witness helps expose unspeakable crimes.

programme.title: Police 911 Michigan

programme.title: Still a Mystery
programme.desc: The body of a young musician turns up in an alley and police soon learn shes been spending time with an older man.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates
programme.desc: A quiet rural town is rocked when two middle school girls go on a hike and are later found murdered; when police discover one of the girls may have recorded her killer with her cellphone, the race is on to find the mysterious man.

programme.title: Diabolical: Deadly Love
programme.desc: Air Force veteran Michael Giles disappears along with his savings of $206,000 and detectives find a that Kwaneta Harris has been draining his accounts.

programme.desc: A college student disappears, and her family launches a desperate investigation to find her; what they discover changes their lives forever.

programme.title: On the Case With Paula Zahn
programme.desc: A murdered teenagers body mysteriously turns up in the rural Iowa countryside. Paula Zahn examines the smallest of clues, which may turn out to be the key to bringing her killer to justice.

programme.title: Mean Girl Murders
programme.desc: In the cutthroat world of Vegas showgirls, Debbie Flores Narvaez isnt afraid to ruffle feathers as she climbs to the top, but when Debbie goes missing, investigators must wonder if a jealous dancer or a romantic rival took her out.

programme.title: Mean Girl Murders
programme.desc: Minnesota teen mum Cheyenne Clough is over the hallway dramas and petty jealousies of high school. She finds acceptance in a new crowd by moving in with two gals and their boyfriends. But after Cheyenne gets her own beau, distrust leads to death.

programme.title: A Killer Among Friends
programme.desc: When a middle schooler vanishes without a trace, suspicion quickly falls on the new circle of older friends shes been spending time with. As family and friends dig deeper, secrets unravel, and the line between loyalty and betrayal starts to blur.

programme.title: Portals to Hell
programme.desc: Jack and Heather Taddy are in Nebraska to investigate a museum full of cursed artefacts. The building is rumoured to be haunted by demonic forces.

programme.title: Paranormal Lockdown
programme.desc: Katrina and Nick investigate an abandoned haunted asylum in West Virginia. On their third night of confinement, what they capture can only be described as utterly terrifying.

programme.title: Kindred Spirits
programme.desc: A menacing ghost frightens an 11-year-old girl. Is a disturbed grave of a giant Native American man at the root of the haunting?

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: According to a 2022 YouGov survey, more than a third of us in the UK feel tired most or all of the time. So with stress, nutrition and our sleep patterns all playing their part, what steps can we take to beat fatigue? Datshiane Navanayagam investigates.

programme.title: How to Clean Up for Cash
programme.desc: Josie embarks on her first secret mission in Somerset. Armed and ready with expert valuer Dan and professional organiser Jacqui, the gang help Mel whos desperate for her husband of three years, Paul, to rent out his house and move in with her - so his clutter has to go. However, this is no easy task as Paul is a private investigator! Making sure their mission remains top secret Josie lures Paul out of his house to shop for vintage bargains on a fake show.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running prime-time drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole, Simon OBrien and Max McMurdo travel across the UK to find unique items lost in peoples homes to fix up for a profit. This episode features an old cattle feeder.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first to host are husband-and-wife team Paul Billington and Flora Loizou from Silverwood Lodge in Cambridgeshire, the only couple in this weeks competition. As the hosts show their three female guests to their rooms, Caroline, owner of The Dean Bed and Breakfast, gives a frosty response to the fact that they dont live on site, while Sue from Seashells Guesthouse is left in hysterics.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The group are off to Great Yarmouth to stay in Sues dog friendly Seashells Guesthouse. After revealing that she allows dogs to sleep in the beds during the last BandB visit, no one knows quite what to expect. As soon as they pull up, all the guests are blown away by the doggy-themed exterior. The interior is equally memorable, with Paul and Flora finding themselves in the Cappuccino Room and Caroline in the Poppy Room.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: In Bath, forthright American ex-banker Nancy Hampson isnt everybodys favourite person. Theres trouble from the off for her at The Old Court Guesthouse.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests head to Scotland to stay with final host Caroline at The Dean Bed and Breakfast. The picturesque house, its two acres of gardens and breath-taking views across the Firth of Forth immediately win everyone over. However, rogue underwear in the drawers and cobwebs on the curtains are touches that the guests arent so keen on.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: There are tensions, tempers and tears as the group reconvene for one last time to thrash out the feedback, reveal the payments and announce the winner in the competition to be named best value BandB of the week. Paul and Flora from Silverwood Lodge are fired up about Nancys comments and a tussle over TVs and hot water leads to accusations of lying and game playing.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first host, bargain-hunter Heidi, serves up an adventurous Asian menu. But will her cheap meat and a game that mimics her shopping habits satisfy her guests?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sylvia serves up Mediterranean seafood and Spanish chicken on a fancy dress 80s night featuring a show-stopping Madonna tribute and dance moves nobody was expecting.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Showbiz journalist Benjy serves up airline-inspired grub on a night where he captains a plane taking his guests to Gran Canaria - and his gran is the glam cabin crew.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Guide leader Nicky plans a menu inspired by her dream visit to Asia, on a night featuring complicated cooking, geographical grub and a newspaper fashion show.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the last night in Hertfordshire, party-loving Mark hosts a Greek evening, with saganaki and lamb kleftiko on the menu. Marks kept his cards close to his chest all week, so his guests are keen to find out more about him, on a night that ends with an action-packed obstacle course around his kitchen, and an emotional moment at the dinner table.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The Pavilion is a London `fashion and rock n roll hotel, owned by former pop singer and Lamborghini lover, Danny Karne. His guests are wowed by the flashy sports car parked out the front and impressed by the colourful decor in the themed rooms, but some guests think they are all style, no substance.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The Fox and Duck in Therfield, Hertfordshire, is a country inn owned by John Luce and partner, Pat Sutton. John and Pat take their guests to the local golf club for a lesson in pitch and putt. The group appear to bond with lots of cheeky banter, and later they end the night with a sing song.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The New Inn is in Newbridge-on-Wye and is run by Dave and Debbie Lang. As a keen pig breeder, Dave likes to get his guests in the mood for breakfast with quite a few piggy ornaments. As well as running a pub, he owns the on-site butchers shop, and shows and also races pigs.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to Robert and Kerstins establishment Westleigh Farm, in Somerset. Upon inspecting the rooms Danny finds dust and cobwebs and some wrong hangers, but theres praise elsewhere as Debbie comments on how big the showers are and John and Pat run some successful milk tests. Later, theres a beekeeping lesson and snooker, and some generous helpings for breakfast.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: As the group meets for the last time, the payments are revealed and the winning BandB announced, but old tensions surface and there are heated exchanges around the table.

programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil help more owners decide whether to love their home or sell it. In East Grinstead, Lucy and Lynton are at loggerheads over their three-bed semi.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: 59-year-old Becky is rushed to St George after a suspected stroke, and the doctors are concerned that she could have had a bleed on the brain. 19-year-old Georgia is brough to AandE with suspected spinal injuries after falling 20 feet on the concrete.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Betting shop owner Les is rushed to St Georges with lacerations on his head after a car accident. Plumber Cambelle has an infected cyst on her bottom that requires surgery, and a retired doctor Patrick is brought in after falling from his horse.

programme.desc: Seth Knorr performs a night of standup comedy joking about global warming and family budgets.

programme.title: Chris Cope: Baby Face
programme.desc: Comedian Chris Cope performs a standup set.

programme.title: Chris Young: Buoyant Mon
programme.desc: Chris Young takes real-life experiences and turn them into laughs that maintain a clean but funny appearance.

programme.title: Dennis Regan: Dennisland
programme.desc: Dennis Regan performs his signature brand of observational comedy.

programme.title: Andrew Hobbs: A Joke Unless it Works
programme.desc: Andrew has, as an adult, been bullied by a healthcare professional or popular middle school girl.

programme.title: Alex Velluto: Spurious
programme.desc: Like Rocky Balboa, Alex Velluto is the son of an Italian immigrant and brings the same intensity, will, and drive to the comedy stage.

programme.title: Rodney Norman: Santa
programme.desc: Rodney Norman tells what its like to live in this world as Saint Nick.

programme.title: Adam Hess: Seahorse
programme.desc: Adam Hess presents a frivolous, rapid fire display of freaky family findings and sexual awakenings with hyperactive hilarity.

programme.title: Njambi McGrath: Black Black
programme.desc: Comedian grannies, mud-floor comedy clubs, white-face audiences, broke aristocrat orgies with angry Afrikaans, soldiers in search of stiffies, African matriarchs objectifying themselves and civil disobedience are the perfect ingredients for mirth.

programme.title: Dylan Brody: Driving Hollywood
programme.desc: Brody explores his experiences of heartbreak and hope, and recalls a lifetime of professional disappointment and disillusionment on the drive to and from what might just be the most important meeting of his career.

programme.title: Sean McLoughlin: Kamikaze
programme.desc: The ever angry Sean McLoughlin is given free reign to fully explore his darkly witty world views.

programme.title: Andrew Maxwell: Showtime
programme.desc: Maxwell explores recent political and personal disasters using mischievous improv and a healthy dose of bigot baiting.

programme.title: Juliette Burton: Butterfly Effect
programme.desc: A moving, inspirational hour of stigma-busting gags, multimedia projection, hidden camera filming and animation.

programme.title: Ben Van der Velde: Fablemaker
programme.desc: Ben Van der Velde raids the library of his fantastical imagination to create thrillers, romances, ghost stories and more original freewheeling tales thatve never been told before and never will be again.

programme.title: Juliette Burton: Defined
programme.desc: Juliette Burton goes on her journey of self discovery (and mental health recovery) in asking: why cant we be everything at once?

programme.title: Jasmine Ellis: Nobodys Queen
programme.desc: Jasmine Ellis discusses being a newly married millennial and why she could be the best or worst employee in the world depending on the stars.

programme.title: Nazareth: American By Choice
programme.desc: Award winning Comedian Nazareth performs in a stand-up special.

programme.title: Tim Cavanagh: The Joys of Uncle Dumb
programme.desc: With a companion guitar, Tim Cavanagh asks for a wake up call, points out another unhappy marriage, finds a human music stand, and plays a possum game.

programme.title: Andy Woodhull: Worlds Greatest Stepdad
programme.desc: This special premiered on Comedy Central after an appearance on Conan.

programme.title: Chris Strait: Laugh Till You Run Out of Air

programme.title: Joey Callahan: The Notorious F.O.D.
programme.desc: Comedian Joey Callahan performs standup.

programme.title: Danny Johnson: The Chusky Life

programme.title: Orlando Baxter: Glorified Baby Sitter

programme.desc: Josh uses his humour and easy-going stage presence to entertain.

programme.title: Rodney Norman: Santa
programme.desc: Rodney Norman tells what its like to live in this world as Saint Nick.

programme.title: Bob Zany: I Think I Handled That Well

programme.desc: Robert Mac returns to the Dry Bar stage with less smartness, less subtleness, and even more silliness.

programme.sub-title: Nicola Hughes and Fiona Bone; Dale Cregan

programme.desc: An escaped inmate murders Debra Johnson, a highly respected Correctional Administrator, at her home outside the prison. With the public at risk, police scour through surveillance footage for clues to hunt him down before he strikes again.

programme.title: Faking It: Tears of a Crime
programme.desc: Looking at the phenomenon of high-profile criminal cases where people publically claim they are innocent about a crime they have committed.

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: Air Force veteran Roger Harris and his wife Donna, a nurse, built their dream home in Florida, but just after the birth of their second son, a vile secret threatens their sweet, simple life; someones hatching a plot that no one ever saw coming.

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: Family comes first to grandparents Ron and Kay Wilkson, though they may not have much money, their growing brood always knows how to make their own fun. However, something evil is prowling close to home and not everyone will make it out alive.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here
programme.desc: Pam Jacobs reveals the dark truth about her father. Growing up, she never realised he was a fugitive suspected of the murders of various coin shop owners.

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.sub-title: Michelle Stewart; John Wilson; Helen Hill
programme.desc: When 17-year-old Michelle Stewarts first relationship came to an end, she was looking forward to moving on and exploring the exciting opportunities that lay ahead. But ex-boyfriend John Wilson had other ideas, failing to accept the break-up. He wanted to make sure that if he couldnt have Michelle, nor could anyone else. When young mum Helen Hills husband didnt come home after a night out, panic set in, but nothing could have prepared her for the truth behind his absence.

programme.desc: When Rhonda Daugherty vanishes, detectives turn to the public for information. A tip leads to surveillance footage from a hotel that uncovers a journey of deceit.

programme.desc: An escaped inmate murders Debra Johnson, a highly respected Correctional Administrator, at her home outside the prison. With the public at risk, police scour through surveillance footage for clues to hunt him down before he strikes again.

programme.title: A Killers Mistake
programme.desc: A look at how detectives nailed wife-killer Nat Fraser, revealing how the scheming murderer botched the seemingly perfect plot.

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: Tammy Gardner loves being a mum and running a daycare out of her suburban Delaware home; she also loves a night out on the town; what starts as innocent fun leads this 40-year-old down a dark path of jealousy and deadly rage.

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: Police officer Albert Pacheco and his wife Cathy live Hawaiis Big Island, raising four kids in the bosom of their close-knit church community; what looks like a household steeped in traditional goodness is hiding something evil.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here
programme.sub-title: He Almost Got Away With It
programme.desc: The heart-stopping accounts from people sharing their homes with loved ones, who become killers.

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Judge Robert Rinder investgates two serious cases, including how the Border Force and the National Crime Agency work together to carry out the UKs biggest ever drugs bust.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates
programme.desc: In 2022, the execution of a rising cyclist the weekend before a race in Texas sparks a police probe that uncovers jealousy, betrayal, and obsession.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates
programme.desc: Friends are stunned when an esteemed criminal law professor is executed in his garage. Investigators follow a series of leads that take them on a state-wide hunt.

programme.title: Forensic Detectives
programme.desc: Tiny evidence helps bring to justice a murderer who stabbed two young women to death. Plus, the search for the identity of a woman found decapitated.

programme.title: Forensic Detectives
programme.desc: When clever killers try to cover their tracks, investigators turn to specially trained dogs to hound them.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: Family man Dr David Cornbleet is brutally murdered in his office. Police must rely on CCTV images of man with a concealed face to lead them to the killer.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: When Christie Wilson disappears after a night out, CCTV cameras reveal her gambling with a mysterious man. Can this footage unlock the secrets of what happened?

programme.desc: Los Angeles Police and Compton Police begin to advocate to Las Vegas Police that the shooting is a Compton Problem.

programme.title: Tupac: Cover-Up
programme.desc: The Vegas Shakur murder investigation saw LAPD detectives Russell Poole and Greg Kading, removed from the case amid credibility issues.

programme.title: Tupac: Cover-Up
programme.desc: Kadings Murder Rap thrusts the False Narrative into the spotlight, introducing Keefe D as a key participant in the 1996 shooting.

programme.title: Tupac: Cover-Up
programme.desc: By 2018, Las Vegas police were reinvigorated by Keefe-Ds alleged TV confessions supporting Kadings Murder Rap story.

programme.title: Killing John Lennon
programme.desc: On a cold dark winters night on the Upper West Side of Manhattan, New York City, one man made a fateful decision that changed the world forever. It was December 8th 1980 and John Lennon was about to take his final steps.

programme.title: The Day That James Dean Died
programme.desc: James Deans friends and family tell of his love for his controversial racing career.

programme.title: The Trayvon Hoax: Unmasking the Witness Fraud That Divided America
programme.desc: A look into the trial of George Zimmerman for the murder of Trayvon Martin.

programme.title: Who Killed Lt. Van Dorn?
programme.desc: The wife of the late Lt Wes Van Dorn seeks an explanation of her husbands death after the helicopter he was piloting crashes off the coast of Virginia.

programme.title: Who Killed Gandhi?
programme.sub-title: Symbol of India Past and Future
programme.desc: The paths of three men crossed during these volatile times, resulting in a violent murder that would change the course of history. Each was dedicated to an independent mother India but separated by diametrically opposed views that would lead to an epic battle for the heart and soul of a nation.

programme.title: Final 24
programme.desc: Its July 14, 1997, and Gianni Versace, probably the most famous fashion designer in the world, is kicking back at his Florida mansion, but in just 24 hours he will be dead. With exclusive interviews with his intimates, combined with archive footage and dramatic reconstruction, `Final 24 maps out Versaces last day alive and the dramatic events that led to his brutal murder at the hands of a serial killer.

programme.title: Final 24
programme.desc: Its 12 June 1994, and vibrant, funny, beautiful Nicole Brown Simpson appears to have it all. Rich, with a great body and two adorable children, the ex-wife of football legend OJ Simpson is living the Hollywood dream. In 24 hours, though, the dream will be brutally shattered, as Nicole will be dead and her ex-husband will stand accused in the murder trial of the century.

programme.title: Final 24
programme.desc: 18 April 1993, and one of the most notorious religious leaders in the United States, David Koresh, along with 83 of his followers, has been trapped inside a Waco, Texas, compound for over 50 days, unwilling to surrender and surrounded by the police authorities. The siege is the longest modern-day standoff in American law enforcement history. In 24 hours, however, it will all be over, and Koresh and 73 others will be dead.

programme.title: The Manson Murders
programme.desc: The Manson Family, led by Charles Manson, committed a set of crimes that terrified the world and brought the flower power movement to a halt. Charles Milles Manson was an American criminal and musician who led the Manson Family, a cult based in California.

programme.title: Killed Walking Home
programme.desc: Every three days, a woman is killed in the UK. These women could be sisters, daughters and friends. Although there is a lead that breaks open the case, what comes in light of the investigation is one that leaves an everlasting scar on the families.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.sub-title: Death of a Mail Order Bride
programme.desc: A mother disappears after she drops her children off with her ex-husband. He evades police and is found with thousands of dollars and his passport.

programme.title: Finding Dawn
programme.desc: An investigation into the estimated 500 Aboriginal women in Canada who went missing or were murdered over the past 30 years.

programme.title: Catch My Killer
programme.desc: Connie Hevener and Carolyn Hevener Perry are brutally killed while working at an ice cream shop.

programme.title: Catch My Killer
programme.desc: Donna Fowlers disappearance isnt her first, but its her last. Years later, Stacy Belcher vanishes too. Two families face the horror of a long-time monster.

programme.title: Catch My Killer
programme.desc: When Marlene Major disappears in 1980, her family suspects she ran off to escape her abusive husband. Her daughter is determined to learn the truth and seek justice for her mother.

programme.title: Catch My Killer
programme.desc: Marilyn Kananen has vanished and her children are afraid that their abusive father has returned.

programme.title: Catch My Killer
programme.desc: Two years after a 13-year-old girl was taken from her home, her skull is found.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Who is the best portrait artist in the UK and Ireland? Thats the question that this programme tries to answer. The show, with its judging panel, travels to London, Glasgow, Dublin and Cardiff to hold competitions to find each citys top portrait artist. Those four artists then travel to Paris for further inspiration and development, producing one further work during their two days in Frances capital city. The four finalists work is then displayed in Londons National Portrait Gallery for a period. In addition to being considered for further projects by the gallery, the winner is awarded a £10,000 commission to produce a portrait of author Hilary Mantel that will become part of The British Librarys permanent art collection.

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected

programme.title: Rampage
programme.desc: Primatologist Davis Okoye shares an unshakable bond with George, an extraordinarily intelligent silverback gorilla thats been in his care since birth. When a genetic experiment goes wrong, it causes George, a wolf and a reptile to grow to a monstrous size. As the mutated beasts embark on a path of destruction, Okoye teams up with a discredited genetic engineer and the military to secure an antidote and prevent a global catastrophe.

programme.desc: Brixton Lorr is a cybernetically enhanced soldier who possesses superhuman strength, a brilliant mind and a lethal pathogen that could wipe out half of the worlds population. programme.title: Mission: Impossible - The Final Reckoning

Burning rubber all over the city with his ex-con friend, he attempts to deliver the crook to the FBI without crashing and being blown to pieces in one of the many explosions.
